items.subject: Adam Nelson left a comment in "Update on DBRG priorities"
items.body_text:
[Adam Nelson]
Update on DBRG priorities.docx 

 
Adam Nelson added a comment

I have some thoughts on this. I'd like to extract Expert Services from Cloud platform... 

I'd most likely look at this like:
* Infrastructure (delivers the commodity of physical infra)
* Cloud (we either keep this as hyperscaler) OR we include private cloud
* Expert services (management tiers + PM + PS)

…nada and US East Coast especially), with secondary presence in the UK.
#6 Develop Ideal Customer Engagement (ICE) framework
@marc-alex to connect with JP. Also @Adam has an operations centric view.
Updates
Next Steps

#7 Simplify P&L - — eliminate LOB breakout, align to investor view
Notes: @Sarah and @Ian met last week to prepare. 
Updates
•Ian and Sarah have had 2 sessions to review and align. 
•Sarah to schedule time with @Peter and @Pierre to review as relates to valuation model DONE last Fri… 
Go to comment 

Why am I receiving this notification from Office?

 

This email is generated through Aptum's use of Microsoft 365 and may contain content that is controlled by Aptum.

items.subject: Re: Aptum/ThinkOn
items.body_text:
[Ian Rae]
Great to hear JP, will get some times for next week for an hour together.

Ian

Ian Rae
President & CEO
+1 (514) 944-4008
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: JP Rosato (Consultant) <JP.Rosato@digitalbridge.com>
Sent: April 14, 2026 13:33
To: Ian Rae <ian.rae@aptum.com>; David Pistacchio (Consultant) <David.Pistacchio@digitalbridge.com>; Adam Nelson <adam.nelson@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <sblanchard@cloudops.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Craig <craig.mclellan@thinkon.com>
Subject: Fw: Aptum/ThinkOn 
 

Ian and team,

Had a very good call with Craig,  following an intro from Ian, around our current relationship and the potential opportunity to expand that with Aptum and perhaps further.

Like to schedule a session next week to dive into the topics below and design and define an actionable plan to drive this relationship further.

LMK what works next week and thanks.

JP Rosato
Operating Partner

From: Craig <craig.mcLellan@thinkon.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 11:59 AM
To: JP Rosato (Consultant) <JP.Rosato@digitalbridge.com>
Subject: Re: Aptum/ThinkOn 
 
JP,

For our conversation - I had these topics/thoughts

Broadcom consolidation.  We are seeing a resurgence of Broadcom with VCF 9 and our backoffice expertise is extensive.  Does it make sense to leverage that more so than trying to establish a multi-hypervisor approach.  Could this even be used across more portfolio companies?

We want to accelerate our growth in the UK and does it make sense to consolidate our efforts over there into one team

We've lock in storage pricing for the next 4 years PLUS we have about 8,000 hard drives that would be really strong object storage platform that we could collectively leverage include vendor co-selling

There are probably other

Subject: Aptum/ThinkOn

items.subject: Adam Nelson replied to a comment in "Update on DBRG priorities"
items.body_text:
[Adam Nelson]
Update on DBRG priorities.docx 

 
You left a comment

@Sarah Blanchard is this with you or @Adam Nelson

 
Adam Nelson replied

Jorge is still involved from a process perspective and ensuring the data pipelines are coherent But IMO, this probably belongs w/ Sarah to make sure that the LOBs are properly attributed. PS should go into the expert services LOB and then we just need to add some delineators for advisory vs. execute. 

… support of the transition plan.

#3 Link Advisory → Execute engagements → MRR in Salesforce
Working with Chameleon)Consulting vs professional services -need to make sure these are tagged so we know what we have sold and we are tracking them appropriate and linking to the related execution and MRR (Reach out to Jorge today to work with Chameleon)
Updates
•Jorge engaged with Chameleon
•Jorge creating a weighted backlog of related requests in JIRA
Next Steps
•Complete the Salesforce cleanup to cle… 
Go to comment 

Why am I receiving this notification from Office?

 

This email is generated through Aptum's use of Microsoft 365 and may contain content that is controlled by Aptum.

items.subject: Heading to IT Nation Evolve Q2 – Let’s Connect!
items.body_text:
[Client Success Team at ScalableOS]
Join us in Chicago for meaningful MSP discussions. ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­   

 

Hello Adam! 
 
The ScalableOS team will be at IT Nation Evolve Q2 in Chicago from April 29 – May 1, 2026, and we’re looking forward to connecting with partners who will be there. 
 
These events are always a great chance to step away from the day-to-day and have meaningful conversations with peers across the MSP community.  
 
Our CEO will be onsite throughout the week and always welcomes the opportunity to meet in person. Whether we’ve had the chance to connect before or this would be our first time meeting at Evolve, we’d enjoy taking some time to talk and learn more about what your team is focused on this year. 
 
If you’d like to set up some time while on-site, feel free to reach out to your CSM, and they can help coordinate. 

Click Here to Contact Your CSM 

 
 
 
 

ScalableOS, PO Box 98257, Des Moines, WA 98198 
Unsubscribe Manage preferences

items.subject: Re: Intro
items.body_text:
[Charles Louis Guerin]
Hi Adam!
We certainly have CMC in common -- you reminded me of many years ago when we made a documentation delivery system for CMC’s tablet EFBs for Jean-Marie Bégis (was he still there when you worked there?) Feels like a lifetime ago.
Would be great to trade notes!  I see you’re running operations, which must be exciting at Aptum! I’ve got my operations hat on at the moment to see how to best get AI plugged in to OBDS to aid our operations (and not scare the staff too much 😊) 

Over the past year I’ve been deep into Claude Code and have watched it mature quite a bit. It went from really annoying to really useful in a very short timeframe.  We used it to build out a full training system for Airbus — delivering SCORM packages through our Aviation Docs platform. That platform now includes a complete LMS now, with grading and the ability for pilots to take courses anywhere, online or offline, and is just deploying now to the training centers.
What made the difference for us was blending traditional senior programmer development with a bit of “vibe coding” - using AI where it accelerates things but still keeping strong engineering control where it mattered. Having senior devs review AI code proved to be a “MUST”.
The part I find most valuable is using AI for “critique passes” - reviewing an entire project’s code against security requirements, best practices, structure, optimization and DevOps. Those checks are fast and surprisingly thorough, which changes how you think about QA. It’s allowed our tiny company to “up its game”
Since January, I’ve been pushing it internally. We’re using Claude Code to build a system to surface all the information that’s usually buried across SharePoint, project boards, time tracking, and quality systems — and make it accessible in a coherent way. Everyone who needs context will now have it, without hunting through 6 systems.
We’re also using AI to lower the barrier for non-developers. For example, our project managers will soon be able to run structured OWASP-style checks on code, follow guided ISO procedures for testing and project initiation, and work through their ISO processes with AI-driven scripts. And we made it seriously a “push button” affair -

Underneath, we’re tying systems together via APIs and MCP-style services, so it behaves like a single environment and it’s amazingly robust. We have it as a desktop app, web app, mcp server where you can define the company’s roadmap, plan projects, specify requirements, define phases and activities, decide whether humans or AI is your best option, enter all the typical ISO information (Risk/Opportunities/Controls/Infrastructure Definitions, Documentation), GDPR, a simple CRM (for now) and the pretty sophisticated  AI Task launcher which can launch from the web or from desktops and do crazy things like – audit every application in the entire company as a batch process.  
Ian mentioned this starts to look a bit like Venta, which is true for the Iso side for sure – we like to save money 😊 … but the intent is to go way further.  The goal is – allow us to concentrate on making our products and service guarantees – and not worry as much about the details.

Hopefully this will let the team focus on delivering features while the system handles the process overhead. I want our staff to be able to step back and operate with a clearer, higher-level view of their role, while AI handles much of the detail work. We spend about 35% of our time testing software over and over – every hotfix, every iteration requires full regressive testing. AI automation for that will cut that down to 10% and free up my staff to concentrate on customer needs, where we need to grow our value.
If you’re doing anything with Claude or Claude Code, I’d be VERY interested to compare notes. I know very few people using it seriously. We’ve got a couple of developers on it now, but adoption is mixed, especially with more experienced programmers (who either don’t trust AI or just want to convince me they’re better than AI). To give you an idea of craziness – we have a Sentry.io 

In any case this ERP system I call “Bienveilla” will roll out across the company in the next few weeks. If you’re doing operations AI, might be possible collaboration to help both our operations succeed.
On Thursday this week, my entire afternoon is free. If you were available any time on Thursday, would love to talk! Next week is fine too, Tuesday or Thursday.
I look forward to meeting you!

All the best!

Charles Guerin, FRAeS
President

Phone:  +1 (450) 476-1420
Fax:      +1 (450) 988-1116
https://ovou.me/cguerin

            Air Safety, Delivered.
Checklists | Aviation Docs | Tech Pubs
    Reliable solutions for safer skies.
 
 
From: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 7:45 AM
To: Charles Louis Guerin <cguerin@obds.com>
Cc: Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Intro

IRONSCALES couldn't recognize this email as this is the first time you received an email from this sender Adam.Nelson @ aptum.com
 

You don't often get email from adam.nelson@aptum.com. Learn why this is important 

Nice to meet you, Charles.

 

Small world. I was on the EFB/AIS team at CMC Electronics before joining Ian at CloudOps, so this is right in my wheelhouse. 

 

I'm extremely interested to hear how you're using Claude (and other LLMs) in the "real world". Happy to find some time this week or next if you're available.

 

Thanks,

 

Adam Nelson

Vice President, Operations
+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Sent: April 13, 2026 19:44
To: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Cc: cguerin@obds.com <cguerin@obds.com>
Subject: Intro

 

Hey Adam

 

Don't know if you've ever met Charles before. He is the CEO at OBDS which does electronic flight bag, checklist and maintenance software for aircraft including Cessna and Airbus.

 

I was talking with Charles the other day and he gave me a demo of the compliance software he created with Claude, and I was thinking with your avionics background and your recent successes with Claude, you should probably chat.

 

Ian

 

Ian Rae

President & CEO

items.subject: RE: Master Communication Agreement from Bell
items.body_text:
[Matthew Carter]
Hi Adam,
 
PDF 1: 36-month term with a 2.5% annual price escalation after the first 12-months. Includes a 1-year auto-renewal clause, $2M CGL insurance requirement, and a 15-day obligation to remove equipment upon termination. 
PDF 2: Provides the vendor the right to relocate the customer with 60 days’ notice. Includes a 1-year auto-renewal clause (60-day notice). 
PDF 3: Includes a 36-month auto-renewal clause (60-day notice). 
 
There are also technical provisions outside my area of expertise.
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Subject: Re: Master Communication Agreement from Bell

Let me know if you see anything blatant that we need to get in front of (if you can please).

Adam Nelson

+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Cc: Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Master Communication Agreement from Bell 

 

Hi Adam,
These are standard terms; there’s an auto renewal clause for each schedule under this MSA and they require 60-day nonrenewal notice, 30-day early termination, with 50% of remaining fees due.
 
If the contract value is modest, our ability to redline may be limited.
 
Based on this, I’m good if you sign. 
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain
+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Thanks,

items.subject: Grey to Green - Smart, Sustainable Alternatives to OEM-Only Strategies
items.body_text:
[DTC Telecom]
Discover cost-effective second-user, green market telecoms solutions from DTC. It's no longer second choice - it's strategic advantage and smarter procurement. ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­   

 

Grey to Green - The Perception is Changing

Hi Adam
 
The perception is changing.
 
What was once considered the "grey market" is now recognised as a smart, sustainable alternative to OEM-only strategies.
 

 

 
In the re-branded "Green Market" Second-user telecoms equipment is helping operators:
Reduce costs
Shorten lead times
Support sustainability goals
Keep projects on track
Ensure critical spares levels are maintained 
It's no longer second choice - it's strategic advantage and smarter procurement. 
 
Ready to enter the green market? Get in touch with us today. 

Submit an enquiry 

Schedule a discovery call 

Why Partner with DTC?

 

Asset Recovery - Turning Redundant Equipment into Recovered Cash
DTC provides a fully managed reverse logistics service to help you timely monetise surplus, idle, or end-of-life telecom equipment.

 

Network Spares at a Fraction of the OEM Cost
Access warrantied equipment across all network layers with fast international dispatch and significant savings.
 

 

Engineering Expertise on Demand
From one‑day tasks to long‑term programmes across RAN, IP, DWDM networks.

 

End-to-End Lifecycle Management
From asset recovery to spares, repairs, support and engineering, a single partner maximising ROI.

Submit an enquiry 

Schedule a discovery call 

DTC International Ltd, Park End Works, Croughton, Brackley, Northamptonshire NN13 5LX, United Kingdom, +44 (0) 1869 810 600 
Unsubscribe Manage preferences

items.subject: Is your infrastructure AI-ready or just AI-adjacent?
items.body_text:
[Expedient]
Cut through the hype with Expedient and Nutanix.️ 

Can't see this email? View it in your browser.

 

The infrastructure choices you make now will determine whether your organization leads or lags in the AI economy.
Join Anthony Jackman, Chief Innovation Officer at Expedient, and Jason Langone, Global AI Business Development at Nutanix for an informal coffee chat on April 28 at 10:30 AM ET to focus on practical infrastructure decisions that matter. 

What we'll cover: Cloud rebalancing reality, the AI infrastructure gap, integration with AI CTRL Platform, and real ROI timelines. 
All attendees receive a coffee eGift card and will be entered to win a Breville Espresso Machine. 
​​​​​
Can't attend live? Register anyway to receive the full replay and key takeaways. 
Register Now   →

1 Allegheny Square
Pittsburgh, PA 15212
Learn more about Expedient
 

You are receiving this email because you previously opted in to receive communications from Expedient or its partners.
Click the link below to manage your preferences at any time.
